title: Rework systemd cgroups to keep Conmon and Container in same hierarchy
body:
Have systemd create a slice for each container, and place conmon and the container itself under that slice. This keeps conmon and the container in the same hierarchy, and allows them to be killed together if need be.

Also sets systemd to be the default cgroup manager for libpod containers.

comments.body: Making systemd the default appears to have caused our tests to explode - nothing can contact the systemd socket to request the creation of cgroups.

@rhatdan For the tests, I think we are going to need to do systemd-in-a-container and run the tests as a unit, or possibly run the test container as systemd and then exec the tests into it. I don't think we can get away with not testing with systemd if we're making it default.

Well could we tests parts with the hosts systemd and parts with cgroupfs if we are running in a container.
For now I would like to get this merged and then we can work on different test setups going forward.
@rhatdan The problem with using the host systemd is that the papr tests aren't running in containers, but still can't contact systemd to set up cgroups. Not sure what's going on with that...

Alright, so all tests (even papr) are running in containers, which explains those failures at least.

I'm beginning to experiment with mounting host systemd in the container, as I realized that if we do systemd in a container we'll hit the OCI systemd hook bug.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 66a5bc0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Update: I've determined that the way we're computing paths is incorrect. Systemd doesn't want filesystem-type paths (`system.slice/libpod-$CID/container-$CID/`) but rather hyphen-delineated paths. Trying to make a good algorithm for producing these from arbitrary user-specified systemd parents.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 16ea659) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@mheon Needs a rebase.  Any progress on this?
@rhatdan I'm beginning to have doubts about our approach, might change over to something more similar to what CRI-O is doing.
Going to close this, given we don't want to go this way moving forward. Will submit a smaller version that just includes the pod changes and makes systemd the default.
